mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside the recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output that precedes earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of observations during late-cycle phases, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.5%, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Growing nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a panel regression over a 10-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising residential investment and construction activity, which correlates with broader economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month following a major shock.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 58% of observations during recovery phases, or the signal's out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year industrial production contraction indicates a supply-side recession that reduces corporate output and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of instances where INDPRO YoY is negative, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.04 during recessionary periods.
